claims.evidence_text: the optimal combination of planting density and nitrogen management was found to be 275 plants m -2 with 200 kg ha -1 N. The simulation results suggested that a plant density of 275 plants m -2 with an N application of 200 kg ha -1 increased grain yield, improved nitrogen use, and produced the highest economic return
claims.claim_summary: Optimum durum wheat plant density of 275 plants/m2 under rainfed central-highlands conditions (paired with 200 kg/ha N).

claims.evidence_text: the optimal combination of planting density and nitrogen management was found to be 275 plants m -2 with 200 kg ha -1 N... produced the highest economic return while minimizing environmental risk under rainfed conditions.
claims.claim_summary: Optimum nitrogen rate of 200 kg/ha N for rainfed durum wheat (highest economic return, lowest environmental risk).

claims.evidence_text: For nitrogen fertilizer, two split applications, the first one-third N fertilizer was applied at planting, and the remaining (two-thirds N) dose was applied 21 days after crop planting. A full dose of phosphorus fertilizer was applied uniformly at planting.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat N applied in two splits: one-third at planting and two-thirds at 21 days after planting.

claims.evidence_text: The rate of 200 kg ha -1 and under all plant densities produced AUE ranging from 24.7 to 28.2% (average 27%)
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat agronomic N-use efficiency at 200 kg/ha N ranged 24.7-28.2% (avg ~27%).

claims.evidence_text: The plot size was 3 m x 3 m (9 m 2 ) and consisted of 16 rows, spaced 20 cm apart... The seeds were sown with a row spacing of 20 cm using a hand drill.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat sown at 20 cm row spacing.

claims.evidence_text: The model predicted the dates from sowing to anthesis with a 0 (zero) difference and dates from sowing to maturity with a difference of 2 days (107 and 109 dates) between the simulated and measured values
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat (cv. Utuba) reached maturity around 109 days from sowing in the central highlands of Ethiopia.

claims.evidence_text: The model accurately simulated GY and HI, with a simulated/measured GY of 4346/4373 kg ha -1 and HI of 0.38/0.37% under normal plant density (225 kg ha -1 ) and nitrogen fertilizer (200 kg ha -1 ) supply.
claims.claim_summary: Measured durum wheat harvest index of 0.37 at optimum plant density and nitrogen supply.

claims.evidence_text: The CERES-Wheat model slightly overpredicted the LAI (5.1 m 2 m -2 ) compared to the measured LAI (3.4 m 2 m -2 ).
claims.claim_summary: Measured durum wheat leaf area index ~3.4 at grain-filling under rainfed conditions.

claims.evidence_text: GY was adjusted to a moisture content of 12.5% wet bases, and a moisture tester was used to test the moisture content of the grain.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat grain yield standardized to 12.5% moisture content (wet basis).
